annotations.label: Axiom of choice (informal)

anchor.snippet: the axiom of choice says that given any collection of non-empty sets

annotations.note: Mathlib's Classical.axiomOfChoice yields a choice function from a family of nonempty types.

annotations.label: Smallest element of finite subsets of naturals

anchor.snippet: the set containing each smallest element is {4, 10, 1}

annotations.note: This is a numeric illustrative example, not a formalizable mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Russell's shoes and socks analogy

anchor.snippet: for any (even infinite) collection of unordered pairs of shoes

annotations.note: An informal pedagogical analogy with no formal counterpart.

annotations.label: Choice function

anchor.snippet: A choice function (also called selector or selection)

annotations.note: A function selecting one element from each nonempty set is exactly the content of Classical.axiomOfChoice.

annotations.label: Axiom of choice (formal axiom box)

anchor.value: Axiom — For any set [MATH] of nonempty s

annotations.note: The existence of a choice function for nonempty sets is Classical.axiomOfChoice (built on Lean's Classical.choice).

annotations.label: Equivalent form via Cartesian product

anchor.snippet: an equivalent form of the axiom of choice is

annotations.note: Classical.nonempty_pi states Nonempty (∀ i, π i) ↔ ∀ i, Nonempty (π i), i.e. the product of nonempty sets is nonempty.

annotations.label: Generalization to indexed families

anchor.snippet: This form implies a more general form where the Cartesian product is of a general indexed family of sets

annotations.note: Classical.nonempty_pi is stated for an arbitrary indexed family of types.

annotations.label: Partition/transversal variant

anchor.snippet: This guarantees for any partition of a set

annotations.note: The choice axiom is present, but there is no dedicated lemma producing a transversal of a partition.

annotations.label: Powerset choice-function variant

anchor.snippet: Another equivalent axiom only considers collections

annotations.note: Per-nonempty-subset choice is available via choice, but no packaged powerset choice-function object exists.

annotations.label: Finite collections need no choice (induction)

anchor.section: Cases where the axiom of choice is not needed
anchor.snippet: The existence of a choice function for a finite collection of nonempty sets can be proved by the principle of finite induction

annotations.note: The nonempty-product lemma covers the finite case, but Mathlib does not isolate a choice-free finite-induction version.

annotations.label: Canonical choice via explicit rule

anchor.section: Cases where the axiom of choice is not needed
anchor.snippet: Another case where the axiom of choice is not needed is when there exists an explicit rule

annotations.note: An illustrative discussion rather than a formal statement.

annotations.label: Well-orderable union gives choice function

anchor.section: Cases where the axiom of choice is not needed
anchor.snippet: In general, if the union of all sets in

annotations.note: A well-order supplies least elements (WellFounded.min), giving a choice function, but no packaged 'well-orderable union → choice' lemma exists.

annotations.label: Subsets of reals require choice

anchor.snippet: As an example where the axiom of choice is required

annotations.note: An illustrative remark about when choice is needed, not a formal statement.

annotations.label: Open interval has no least element

anchor.snippet: does not have a least element

annotations.note: No dedicated lemma stating that an open real interval has no least element was found.

annotations.label: Well-ordering of reals equivalent to choice function

anchor.snippet: If there exists a different ordering of the real numbers which is a well-ordering

annotations.note: The general well-ordering theorem applies to ℝ, but the stated equivalence with a choice function for sets of reals is not a separate decl.

annotations.label: Construction of a non-measurable set

anchor.snippet: Using the axiom of choice, we could pick a single point from each orbit

annotations.note: The Vitali non-measurable set construction is not in Mathlib (docs/1000.yaml points to an external repository).

annotations.label: Banach–Tarski paradox

anchor.snippet: which says that it is possible to decompose the 3-dimensional solid unit ball into finitely many pieces

annotations.note: The Banach–Tarski paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Diaconescu's theorem

anchor.snippet: Diaconescu's theorem shows that the axiom of choice implies the law of excluded middle

annotations.note: Lean derives Classical.em from Classical.choice via Diaconescu's argument; Mathlib re-exports it as the alias em.

annotations.label: AC may fail with urelements (permutation models)

anchor.snippet: It has been known since as early as 1922 that the axiom of choice may fail in a variant of ZF with urelements

annotations.note: Permutation/urelement model results are metamathematics not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Gödel: ZFC consistent if ZF consistent

anchor.snippet: Kurt Gödel showed that the negation of the axiom of choice is not a theorem of ZF

annotations.note: Relative consistency of ZFC over ZF is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Cohen: AC not a theorem of ZF (forcing)

anchor.snippet: Paul Cohen employed the technique of forcing

annotations.note: Forcing and the independence of AC are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: AC independent of ZF

anchor.snippet: Together these results establish that the axiom of choice is logically independent of ZF

annotations.note: The independence of AC from ZF is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Peano-arithmetic statements: ZF iff ZFC

anchor.snippet: are provable in ZF if and only if they are provable in ZFC

annotations.note: This conservativity metatheorem is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: ZF + GCH implies AC

anchor.snippet: ZF plus GCH implies AC, making GCH a strictly stronger claim than AC

annotations.note: The implication GCH → AC over ZF is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Constructibility and GCH imply AC

anchor.snippet: The axiom of constructibility and the generalized continuum hypothesis each imply the axiom of choice

annotations.note: V=L and GCH implying AC are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axiom of global choice

anchor.snippet: there is an axiom called the axiom of global choice that is stronger than the axiom of choice for sets

annotations.note: The class-theoretic axiom of global choice is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Global choice from limitation of size

anchor.snippet: The axiom of global choice follows from the axiom of limitation of size

annotations.note: The axiom of limitation of size and its implications are not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Tarski's axiom stronger than AC

anchor.snippet: Tarski's axiom, which is used in Tarski–Grothendieck set theory

annotations.note: Tarski's axiom (existence of inaccessibles/universes) as a set-theoretic statement is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Trichotomy of cardinalities

anchor.snippet: Trichotomy: The cardinalities of any two sets are comparable with each other

annotations.note: Cardinal forms a LinearOrder (its le_total field), so any two cardinalities are comparable.

annotations.label: Tarski's theorem about choice

anchor.snippet: Tarski's theorem about choice

annotations.note: Cardinal.mul_eq_self proves κ·κ=κ for infinite κ, the AC-equivalent identity of Tarski's theorem.

annotations.label: Every surjection has a right inverse

anchor.snippet: has a right inverse

annotations.note: A surjection has a right inverse via Function.surjInv / Function.Surjective.hasRightInverse.

annotations.label: Disjoint collection has transversal

anchor.snippet: that intersects each member of

annotations.note: Choice is available, but no dedicated lemma produces a transversal meeting each member of a disjoint collection once.

annotations.label: Relation contains a function

anchor.snippet: If a relation

annotations.note: Classical.axiomOfChoice produces a function f with R a (f a) from ∀ a, ∃ b, R a b.

annotations.label: Cartesian product of nonempty family is nonempty

anchor.snippet: The Cartesian product of any indexed family of nonempty sets is nonempty

annotations.note: Classical.nonempty_pi gives nonemptiness of the product of any indexed family of nonempty types.

annotations.label: Maximal disjoint subcollection meeting all members

anchor.snippet: there exists a disjoint subcollection

annotations.note: This Rubin–Rubin choice-equivalent has no dedicated declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Maximal collection with finite intersection property

anchor.snippet: there exists a maximal (under set inclusion) collection

annotations.note: Tukey's lemma gives maximal finite-character families, but the finite-intersection-property phrasing is not a separate decl.

annotations.label: Well-ordering theorem

anchor.snippet: Well-ordering theorem

annotations.note: Zermelo's well-ordering theorem; any type also gets the concrete well-order WellOrderingRel.

annotations.label: Powerset of an ordinal is well-orderable

anchor.snippet: the powerset (i.e., the set of all subsets) of

annotations.note: Special case of the well-ordering theorem applied to a powerset type.

annotations.label: Hausdorff maximal principle

anchor.snippet: Hausdorff maximal principle

annotations.note: IsChain.exists_maxChain (every chain extends to a maximal chain) generalizes Hausdorff's maximality principle.

annotations.label: Antichain principle

anchor.snippet: Antichain principle: Every partially ordered set has a maximal antichain

annotations.note: Only the IsMaxAntichain predicate is defined; the existence theorem 'every poset has a maximal antichain' is absent.

annotations.label: Zorn's lemma

anchor.snippet: Zorn's lemma

annotations.note: Zorn's lemma is zorn_le / exists_maximal_of_chains_bounded.

annotations.label: Kuratowski's lemma

anchor.snippet: Kuratowski's lemma

annotations.note: Kuratowski's maximal-chain lemma is IsChain.exists_maxChain (same statement as Hausdorff's principle).

annotations.label: Tukey's lemma

anchor.snippet: Tukey's lemma

annotations.note: The Teichmüller–Tukey lemma for finite-character families is formalized.

annotations.label: König's theorem

anchor.snippet: König's theorem

annotations.note: Cardinal.sum_lt_prod is König's theorem (sum < product for cardinals).

annotations.label: Every vector space has a basis

anchor.snippet: Every vector space has a basis

annotations.note: Basis.ofVectorSpace builds a basis for any vector space over a division ring.

annotations.label: Krull's theorem (maximal ideal)

anchor.snippet: Krull's theorem

annotations.note: Ideal.exists_maximal gives a maximal ideal in any nontrivial ring.

annotations.label: Group structure on every nonempty set

anchor.snippet: that gives it a group structure

annotations.note: No declaration giving a group structure on an arbitrary nonempty set was found.

annotations.label: Every free abelian group is projective

anchor.snippet: Every free abelian group is projective

annotations.note: Module.Projective.of_free shows free modules (over any ring, hence free abelian groups) are projective.

annotations.label: Baer's criterion (divisible implies injective)

anchor.snippet: Baer's criterion: Every divisible abelian group is injective

annotations.note: Module.Baer.injective is the general Baer criterion; combined with Module.Baer.of_divisible it gives divisible → injective.

annotations.label: Every set is a projective object in Set

anchor.snippet: Every set is a projective object in the category of sets

annotations.note: An instance makes every type a projective object (docstring: 'the axiom of choice says every type is a projective object in Type').

annotations.label: Dual unit ball has an extreme point

anchor.snippet: The closed unit ball of the dual of a normed vector space over the reals has an extreme point

annotations.note: Krein–Milman gives an extreme point of any nonempty compact convex set; the dual unit ball is the special case.

annotations.label: Tychonoff's theorem

anchor.snippet: Tychonoff's theorem : The product of any indexed family of compact topological spaces is compact

annotations.note: isCompact_univ_pi is Tychonoff's theorem for an arbitrary indexed product of compact sets.

annotations.label: Closure of product equals product of closures

anchor.snippet: The closure of the product of any indexed family of subsets of a topological space

annotations.note: closure_pi_set states closure (I.pi s) = I.pi (fun i => closure (s i)).

annotations.label: Maximal consistent superset of first-order sentences

anchor.snippet: is a set of sentences of first-order logic

annotations.note: Maximal theories are defined and obtained from models (completeTheory), but a general Lindenbaum extension lemma is not present.

annotations.label: Löwenheim–Skolem theorem

anchor.snippet: Löwenheim–Skolem theorem

annotations.note: The downward Löwenheim–Skolem theorem is formalized (with an upward companion in Satisfiability).

annotations.label: Every connected graph has a spanning tree

anchor.snippet: Every connected graph has a spanning tree

annotations.note: SimpleGraph.IsTree is defined, but there is no theorem producing a spanning tree of an arbitrary connected graph.

annotations.label: Every small category has a skeleton

anchor.snippet: Every small category has a skeleton

annotations.note: skeleton_isSkeleton exhibits Skeleton C as a skeleton of any category C.

annotations.label: Weakly equivalent small categories are equivalent

anchor.snippet: If two small categories are weakly equivalent, then they are equivalent

annotations.note: No result deriving categorical equivalence from weak equivalence was found.

annotations.label: Freyd adjoint functor theorem

anchor.snippet: Every continuous functor on a small-complete category

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the General Adjoint Functor Theorem via the solution set condition.

annotations.label: Axiom of dependent choice (DC)

anchor.snippet: One example is the axiom of dependent choice

annotations.note: Mathlib assumes full choice and does not define DC as a separate weaker axiom.

annotations.label: Axiom of countable choice (CC)

anchor.snippet: which states that a choice function exists for any countable family of nonempty sets

annotations.note: Mathlib assumes full choice and does not isolate countable choice as a separate weaker axiom.

annotations.label: Ultrafilter lemma (Tarski)

anchor.snippet: every filter is a subset of some ultrafilter

annotations.note: Ultrafilter.exists_le is the ultrafilter lemma: every filter is below some ultrafilter.

annotations.label: Well-orderability by rank below ordinal α

anchor.snippet: for every set S with rank less than α, S is well-orderable

annotations.note: This rank-stratified weak choice principle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Well-orderability by Hartogs number

anchor.snippet: for every set S with Hartogs number less than

annotations.note: This Hartogs-number-stratified weak choice principle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axiom of countable choice (product form)

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Axiom of countable choice : The Cartesian product of any sequence

annotations.note: The ℕ-indexed product statement holds as a special case of Classical.nonempty_pi, though CC is not isolated as an axiom.

annotations.label: Axiom of dependent choice (sequence form)

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Axiom of dependent choice

annotations.note: DC as a sequence-construction principle is not formalized as such in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axiom of choice for finite sets

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Axiom of choice for finite sets

annotations.note: The weak principle of choice for families of finite sets is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Ultrafilter lemma (filter extension)

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Ultrafilter lemma: Every filter on a set

annotations.note: Every filter extends to an ultrafilter via Ultrafilter.exists_le.

annotations.label: Countable union of countable sets is countable

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The union of any countable family of countable sets is countable

annotations.note: Set.countable_iUnion gives countability of a countable union of countable sets.

annotations.label: Infinite set has countable-infinite subset

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Every infinite set has a countable-infinite subset

annotations.note: Built on Infinite.natEmbedding, this gives a countably infinite subset of any infinite set.

annotations.label: Eight definitions of finite set equivalent

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Eight definitions of a finite set are equivalent

annotations.note: Mathlib has Finite/Fintype/Set.Finite but not a packaged equivalence of the eight classical finiteness definitions.

annotations.label: Borel determinacy

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: is a Borel subset of Baire space is determined

annotations.note: Borel determinacy is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Infinite cardinal squared equals itself

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Every infinite cardinal

annotations.note: Cardinal.mul_eq_self proves κ·κ=κ for every infinite cardinal κ.

annotations.label: Vitali theorem (non-measurable sets)

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The Vitali theorem

annotations.note: The Vitali set / existence of a non-measurable set is not in Mathlib (only Vitali covering theory).

annotations.label: Lebesgue-measurable non-Borel sets

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: There exist Lebesgue-measurable subsets of

annotations.note: Existence of Lebesgue-measurable non-Borel sets is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Hausdorff paradox

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The Hausdorff paradox

annotations.note: The Hausdorff paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Banach–Tarski paradox

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The Banach–Tarski paradox

annotations.note: The Banach–Tarski paradox is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Every field has an algebraic closure

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Every field has an algebraic closure

annotations.note: The AlgebraicClosure construction (with IsAlgClosed instance) gives an algebraic closure of any field.

annotations.label: Every field extension has a transcendence basis

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Every field extension has a transcendence basis

annotations.note: exists_isTranscendenceBasis gives a transcendence basis for any faithful ring extension.

annotations.label: Infinite-dimensional spaces have infinite independent subset

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Every infinite-dimensional vector space contains an infinite linearly independent subset

annotations.note: A basis exists (infinite when the space is infinite-dimensional), but the exact phrased statement is not a single declaration.

annotations.label: Stone's representation theorem

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Stone's representation theorem for Boolean algebras

annotations.note: The prime-ideal/filter separation lemma (a key ingredient of Stone duality) is present, but the full representation theorem is not.

annotations.label: Nielsen–Schreier theorem

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The Nielsen–Schreier theorem

annotations.note: subgroupIsFreeOfIsFree shows a subgroup of a free group is free.

annotations.label: Additive groups of R and C isomorphic

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The additive groups of R and C are isomorphic

annotations.note: A ℚ-linear equivalence ℂ ≃ₗ[ℚ] ℝ exists, which in particular is an additive-group isomorphism.

annotations.label: Accumulation point: topological = sequential

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: the topological and sequential definitions of an accumulation point

annotations.note: Accumulation points are characterized via filters/neighborhoods, but the explicit topological=sequential equivalence is not a single decl.

annotations.label: Continuity: topological = sequential

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: For functions between metric spaces, the topological and sequential definitions of continuity are equivalent

annotations.note: continuous_iff_seqContinuous holds for sequential spaces, including metric spaces.

annotations.label: Compactness: topological = sequential

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: For metric spaces, the topological and sequential definitions of compactness are equivalent

annotations.note: isCompact_iff_isSeqCompact equates compactness with sequential compactness (e.g. for metric spaces).

annotations.label: Hahn–Banach theorem

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The Hahn–Banach theorem in functional analysis

annotations.note: exists_extension_norm_eq is the norm-preserving Hahn–Banach extension theorem.

annotations.label: Every Hilbert space has orthonormal basis

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: every Hilbert space has an orthonormal basis

annotations.note: exists_hilbertBasis gives an orthonormal Hilbert basis for any Hilbert space.

annotations.label: Banach–Alaoglu theorem

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The Banach–Alaoglu theorem

annotations.note: WeakDual.isCompact_polar is the Banach–Alaoglu weak-* compactness theorem.

annotations.label: Baire category theorem

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The Baire category theorem

annotations.note: Complete (pseudo)metrizable spaces are Baire spaces; conclusion lemmas are in Topology.Baire.Lemmas.

annotations.label: Discontinuous linear map on infinite-dim TVS

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: On every infinite-dimensional topological vector space there is a discontinuous linear map

annotations.note: Existence of a discontinuous linear map on an infinite-dimensional TVS is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Uniform space compact iff complete and totally bounded

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: A uniform space is compact if and only if it is complete and totally bounded

annotations.note: isCompact_iff_totallyBounded_isComplete states IsCompact s ↔ TotallyBounded s ∧ IsComplete s.

annotations.label: Stone–Čech compactification exists

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Every Tychonoff space has a Stone–Čech compactification

annotations.note: StoneCech with its universal property gives the Stone–Čech compactification.

annotations.label: Urysohn's lemma

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Urysohn's Lemma

annotations.note: Urysohn's lemma is exists_continuous_zero_one_of_isClosed for disjoint closed sets in a normal space.

annotations.label: Tietze extension theorem

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Tietze extension theorem

annotations.note: ContinuousMap.exists_restrict_eq is the Tietze extension theorem for closed subsets.

annotations.label: Existence of partitions of unity

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Existence of partitions of unity

annotations.note: PartitionOfUnity is defined with existence results (via BumpCovering) for normal/paracompact spaces.

annotations.label: Gödel's completeness theorem

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: Gödel's completeness theorem for first-order logic

annotations.note: Mathlib's ModelTheory has no syntactic proof system, so the soundness/completeness theorem is not formalized.

annotations.label: Compactness theorem

anchor.section: Results requiring AC (or weaker forms) but weaker than it
anchor.snippet: The compactness theorem

annotations.note: The first-order compactness theorem is isSatisfiable_iff_isFinitelySatisfiable.

annotations.label: Partition principle

anchor.snippet: Partition principle: Given two sets A and B

annotations.note: The partition principle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Injection or surjection between two sets

anchor.snippet: Given any two sets A and B where B is nonempty

annotations.note: Cardinal comparability (le_total) gives an injection one way; the injection-or-surjection phrasing is not a dedicated decl.

annotations.label: Surjection between two nonempty sets

anchor.snippet: Given any two nonempty sets, a surjection exists

annotations.note: Follows from cardinal comparability plus nonemptiness, but is not stated as a dedicated declaration.

annotations.label: Converse Schröder–Bernstein theorem

anchor.snippet: Converse Schröder–Bernstein theorem

annotations.note: The injection-based Schröder–Bernstein is present; the surjection-based converse (which needs AC) is not formalized.

annotations.label: Weak partition principle

anchor.snippet: Weak partition principle: Given two sets A and B

annotations.note: The weak partition principle is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: No infinite decreasing sequence of cardinals

anchor.snippet: There is no infinite decreasing sequence of cardinals

annotations.note: Cardinal.lt_wf (and WellFoundedLT Cardinal) shows < on cardinals is well-founded, ruling out infinite descent.

annotations.label: Hahn embedding theorem

anchor.snippet: Hahn embedding theorem

annotations.note: hahnEmbedding_isOrderedAddMonoid is the classical Hahn embedding theorem for ordered groups.

annotations.label: Baire property of all reals stronger than ¬AC

anchor.snippet: every set of real numbers has the property of Baire

annotations.note: The statement that every set of reals has the Baire property is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.note: This anti-choice statement is not formalized in Mathlib.

anchor.snippet: There is a function f from the real numbers to the real numbers such that f is not continuous at a

anchor.snippet: There is an infinite set of real numbers without a countably infinite subset

annotations.label: Reals as countable union of countable sets

anchor.section: Statements implying the negation of AC

anchor.snippet: There is a field with two non-isomorphic algebraic closures

annotations.note: This ¬AC consequence is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector space with no basis

anchor.section: Statements implying the negation of AC
anchor.snippet: In all models of ZF¬C there is a vector space with no basis

annotations.note: This ¬AC consequence is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Vector space with bases of different cardinalities

anchor.section: Statements implying the negation of AC
anchor.snippet: There is a vector space with two bases of different cardinalities

annotations.label: Free complete Boolean algebra on countably many generators

annotations.label: Model of ZF¬C with all sets in Rⁿ measurable

anchor.snippet: There exists a model of ZF¬C in which every set in R n is measurable

annotations.label: GCH fails in all models of ZF¬C

anchor.snippet: In all models of ZF¬C, the generalized continuum hypothesis does not hold

annotations.note: This metamathematical statement about ZF¬C models is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Axiom of choice in type theory

anchor.section: Axiom of choice in type theory
anchor.snippet: The axiom of choice states that if for each x of type σ there exists a y of type τ

annotations.note: The type-theoretic choice (∀x ∃y R x y → ∃f ∀x R x (f x)) is exactly Classical.axiomOfChoice / Classical.skolem.

annotations.label: Axiom of choice formulated by Zermelo (1904)

anchor.snippet: The axiom of choice was formulated in 1904 by Ernst Zermelo in order to formalize his proof of the well-ordering theorem

annotations.note: Historical attribution of AC's formulation, not a formal mathematical statement.

annotations.label: Zermelo–Fraenkel set theory with choice (ZFC)

anchor.snippet: Zermelo–Fraenkel set theory with the axiom of choice (ZFC)
